research_summary: Photosynthesis explainers are saturated with 5–13 minute classroom walkthroughs, but a tight 30-second piece can win by myth-busting one persistent misconception—plants don't eat soil—and anchoring the process with a single memorable equation: sunlight + water + CO₂ → sugar + oxygen. Fresh 2024–2025 science (31% higher global CO₂ uptake; quantum energy routing in Photosystem II) gives hooks that feel current without overwhelming a half-minute runtime.

saturated_angles: Full light-dependent + Calvin cycle lecture in one video
Photosynthesis vs cellular respiration equation comparison
Generic 'plants make food from sunlight' without addressing misconceptions
underserved_gaps: 30-second myth-bust: plants gain mass from air (CO₂), not soil
Single-screen animated equation with motion showing inputs → outputs
Fresh science hook: plants absorb more CO₂ than satellite models assumed
Why plants are green (chlorophyll reflects green; absorbs red/blue) in one punchy beat

recent_developments.headline: Plants absorb 31% more CO₂ than previously estimated
recent_developments.relevance: Counterintuitive scale stat for a hook or stat_card without needing deep chemistry

recent_developments.headline: Berkeley Lab maps wandering energy paths inside Photosystem II
recent_developments.relevance: Supports a 'plants are solar engineers' visual metaphor for motion graphics

active_discussions.topic_or_url: Student misconceptions about photosynthesis and respiration
active_discussions.sentiment: Persistent confusion — teachers repeatedly report the same myths across decades
key_quotes: Students believe plants do not respire at all
Many think plants get mass from soil nutrients rather than CO₂

data_points.claim: Global gross primary production is ~157 petagrams of carbon per year — about 31% higher than many satellite-based estimates (~120 PgC/yr)
Up to 99 of 100 photons entering a plant's light-harvesting antenna can be converted to usable chemical energy under optimal conditions
Chlorophyll absorbs red and blue light strongly but reflects green — which is why most land plants appear green
The Calvin cycle (light-independent reactions) typically runs during daylight because it depends on ATP and NADPH from the light reactions — not because it requires darkness
Photosynthesis in chloroplasts splits water to release O₂ while fixing CO₂ into sugars — the reverse of what many students assume about 'plant breathing'

common_questions: Where does a plant actually get its mass — soil, water, or air?
Do plants only photosynthesize during the day and only respire at night?
What is the difference between the 'light' and 'dark' reactions?
Why are plants green if they use sunlight?

misconceptions.myth: Plants get their food/mass mainly from soil and water
misconceptions.reality: Most plant dry mass comes from CO₂ fixed from the air; water and minerals are essential but are not the bulk carbon source
misconceptions.source: SAPS photosynthesis misconception quiz; CBE Life Sciences Education

misconceptions.myth: Photosynthesis is just how plants breathe — the opposite of respiration
misconceptions.reality: Photosynthesis builds sugars using light energy; respiration breaks them down for usable ATP — plants do both
misconceptions.source: Academic reviews of student misconceptions (Amir & Tamir; Köse)

misconceptions.myth: The 'dark reactions' only happen in the dark
misconceptions.reality: The Calvin cycle is light-independent but usually runs in daylight because it consumes ATP/NADPH from the light reactions

audience_insights.knowledge_level: General audience / middle-school biology: knows plants need sun and water, but often confuses gas exchange, mass source, and the two reaction stages
pain_points: Existing explainers are too long for a quick social watch
Diagram-heavy videos feel like homework, not entertainment
Students memorize the equation without understanding what each arrow means

angles_discovered.name: Your Salad Ate the Air
angles_discovered.hook: A tree's weight mostly came from invisible gas — not dirt.
angles_discovered.why_now: Mass-from-soil is the #1 documented misconception; a 30s myth-bust is underserved in short-form
grounded_in: Plants gain mass from CO₂ not soil

angles_discovered.name: Sunlight In, Life Out
angles_discovered.hook: In 30 seconds: sunlight, water, and CO₂ become the oxygen you breathe.
angles_discovered.why_now: Equation-first explainers work when motion shows each reactant entering and products leaving — gap vs long lecture videos

angles_discovered.name: Plants Absorb More Than We Thought
angles_discovered.hook: Scientists just recalculated Earth's lungs — they're 31% hungrier for CO₂.
angles_discovered.why_now: 2024 Nature paper gives a fresh, credible stat hook rare in classroom animations

angles_discovered.name: Why Green? The Color of Waste Light
angles_discovered.hook: Plants are green because they throw away the color they don't need.
angles_discovered.why_now: Chlorophyll absorption/reflection is a single visual beat that answers a universal kid question

visual_references.description: Kurzgesagt-style flat motion graphics with bold icons for inputs/outputs
visual_references.what_works: High contrast, labeled arrows, one idea per scene

visual_references.description: Cross-section leaf diagram with chloroplast zoom
